title: Restore user scripts display on My Scripts and Home pages

requirements.summary: Ensure the Home page correctly fetches and displays all publicly available scripts from the backend, showing script cards with title, description, category, author, and creation date.
acceptanceCriteria: The Home page loads and renders all scripts returned by the backend's public read endpoint.
Each script card displays title, description, category, author, and creation date.
Loading skeletons are shown while scripts are being fetched.
An empty state message is shown if no scripts exist.

file_operations.description: Review and fix the script fetching logic to ensure useGetAllScripts hook is correctly invoked and its data is properly passed to ScriptGrid component. Verify that loading states, error handling, and empty state messaging are working correctly. Ensure the component properly handles the query response from useQueries.ts.

Verify that the component correctly renders ScriptCard components from the scripts array prop, displays loading skeletons during fetch, and shows appropriate empty state messaging when no scripts are available. Ensure no filtering logic is inadvertently hiding scripts.

Verify that all required script properties (title, description, category, author, createdAt) are correctly rendered from the Script type. Ensure the component handles Principal conversion for author display and formats dates appropriately.

requirements.summary: Ensure the My Scripts page correctly fetches and displays all scripts belonging to the authenticated user, with working edit and delete functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated users can navigate to My Scripts and see all their submitted scripts.
Each script entry shows title, description, category, and creation date.
Edit dialog opens pre-populated with the script's existing data and saves changes successfully.
Delete confirmation dialog appears before removing a script, and the list updates after deletion.

file_operations.description: Review and fix the user script fetching logic using useGetScriptsByAuthor hook to ensure it correctly retrieves scripts for the authenticated user's principal. Verify edit dialog pre-population, update mutation calls, and delete confirmation flow. Ensure query invalidation after successful mutations so the list updates properly.

Add or verify useGetScriptsByAuthor hook that calls actor.getScriptsByAuthor with the current user's principal. Ensure the hook is enabled only when actor and identity are available, and properly returns the array of scripts.

requirements.summary: Audit and fix the React Query hooks in useQueries.ts to ensure the script-fetching queries are correctly wired to the backend actor and returning data. Verify that query keys, actor method calls, and data transformations are correct so scripts are not lost or silently failing.
acceptanceCriteria: useQueries.ts script-fetching hooks successfully call the correct backend actor methods.
Query results are properly mapped and returned to consuming components.
No silent errors or empty arrays are returned when scripts exist in the backend.
Console shows no unhandled query errors related to script fetching.

file_operations.description: Audit all script-fetching query hooks (useGetAllScripts, useGetScriptsByAuthor, useGetScript, useFilterScriptsByCategory, useSearchScriptsByTitle) to verify: 1) Query keys are unique and correctly structured, 2) queryFn correctly calls the backend actor method with proper parameters, 3) enabled flags correctly check for actor availability, 4) retry and staleTime settings are appropriate, 5) data transformations (if any) preserve all script data, 6) error handling does not silently suppress failures. Add console logging for debugging if needed.
